      "それはサイコロやその他のランダムな効果を生み出すゲーム要素の欠如を持っています。",
      "それは1901年のヨーロッパ、トルコ、シリア、北アフリカの地図を持っています。",
      "それは56の陸上地域と19の海上地域を持っています。",
      "それは7つの大国に分割された42の陸上地域を持っています。",
      "それはゲーム開始時に14のニュートラルな陸上地域を持っています。",
      "それは補給センターを含む34の陸上地域を持っています。",
      "それは22のホーム補給センターを持っています。",
      "それは12のニュートラルな補給センターを持っています。",
      "それはほとんどの勢力に開始時に2つの軍隊と1つの艦隊を持っています。"
    ],

      "それは交渉フェーズを特徴としています。",
      "それはサイコロやその他のランダムな効果を生み出すゲーム要素の欠如を特徴としています。",
      "それは戦略的、抽象的、そしてシンプルな戦闘解決ルールを特徴としています。",
      "それは戦闘解決にランダムな要素がないことを特徴としています。",
      "それは各軍事ユニットが同じ強さを持つことを特徴としています。",
      "それは郵便プレイに適していることを特徴としています。",
      "それは陸上と海上の力がほぼ同等であることを特徴としています。"
    ],

      "それは1970年代に開催されました。"

    ],

About: It is about forming and betraying alliances with other players and forming beneficial strategies.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with negotiation phases.
ConsistsOf: It consists of negotiation phases and the absence of dice and other game elements that produce random effects.
CreatedBy: It was created by Allan B. Calhamer.
HasA: It has negotiation phases.
It has the absence of dice and other game elements that produce random effects.
It has a map of 1901 Europe plus Turkey, Syria and North Africa.
It has fifty-six land regions and nineteen sea regions.
It has forty-two land regions divided among the seven Great Powers.
It has fourteen neutral land regions at the start of the game.
It has thirty-four land regions containing supply centers.
It has twenty-two home supply centers.
It has twelve neutral supply centers.
It has two armies and one fleet for most powers at the start.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its negotiation phases.
It is characterized by the absence of dice and other game elements that produce random effects.
It is characterized by its strategic, abstract, and simple combat resolution rules.
It is characterized by having no random elements in combat resolution.
It is characterized by each military unit having the same strength.
It is characterized by being well suited to postal play.
It is characterized by having land and sea power be almost equally significant.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the card game of Hearts.
It was influenced by the game of chess.
IsA: It is a strategic board game.
It is a diplomatic simulation game.
It is one of the few early board games that is still played on the web.
KnownFor: It is known for its negotiation phases.
It is known for the absence of dice and other game elements that produce random effects.
It is known for being the first commercially published game to be played by mail.
It is known for being the first commercially published game to generate an active hobby scene with amateur fanzines.
It is known for competitive face-to-face tournaments.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the years leading to the First World War.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
PublishedBy: It was published by Games Research.
It was published by Avalon Hill.
It was published by Hasbro's Avalon Hill division.
It was published by Wizards of the Coast.
It was published by Parker Brothers.
It was published by Waddingtons Games.
It was published by Gibsons Games.
It was published by Asmodée Editions.
PublishedIn: It was published in the United States.
UsedFor: It is used for forming and betraying alliances.
It is used for forming beneficial strategies.
It is used for competitive face-to-face tournaments.
It is used for play by e-mail.
It is used for play on the Internet.
It is used for discussing tactics and strategy.
It is used for sharing intelligence.
It is used for spreading disinformation.
It is used for building units.
UsedIn: It is used in board wargames.
It is used in postal play.
It is used in face-to-face tournaments.
It is used in play by e-mail.
It is used in play on the Internet.
It is used in the game of Hearts.
It is used in the game of chess.
UsedTo: It is used to move units.
It is used to support units.
It is used to hold an area.
It is used to attack a space.
It is used to transport armies.
It is used to build units.
It is used to disband units.
